name: The White House
subject: Meet America’s new Secretary of State, Mike Pompeo
body:
Your 1600 Daily:
| | [](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=f4f1a16f820f2857c0fbf05dd4f1459493352abebe38ebc677457dc07e738ba8456bcfe3546c65baa47b586fdcc12d185d494238af1bbb98) The White House • April 27, 2018
## Driving the Day
| • | Americans won 9 gold medals—and 23 overall—at this year’s Winter Games. President Donald J. Trump congratulated U.S. Olympians this morning. [Watch here. ](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=f4f1a16f820f28576e992926f59c5d04732354a720c7af9717bfb52b379174433b3038d0acbac0e07338b618867bf47fbb23fdf51ff8cd9f)
---|---
• | President Trump welcomes German Chancellor Angela Merkel to Washington today, following Mrs. Merkel’s recent reelection to a fourth term. [Watch live at 1:50 p.m. ET.](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=f4f1a16f820f2857d9905a8c803b212e42bf9729987475f3b3d58594c4ae74ed067a3044946e9c79ab30d005081add55ca0b3814559dd1ed)
## Meet America’s new Secretary of State, Mike Pompeo
**Yesterday, the Senate easily confirmed Mike Pompeo to be our 70th Secretary of State**. Pompeo, who has served as CIA Director since last year, drew unanimous Republican support and earned the votes of several key Democrats. “Considering his distinguished time as CIA Director & his exemplary career in public service, I will vote to confirm Mike Pompeo,” Sen. Joe Manchin (D-WV) tweeted this week.
**Pompeo’s bipartisan confirmation attests to his record**. Newspaper editorial boards across the country have praised his invaluable mix of instincts and experience. “He has invigorated the CIA clandestine service, tried to give Mr. Trump options on North Korea, and has gained the President’s trust,” _The Wall Street Journal_ explained last Wednesday.
**President Trump calls Pompeo a true patriot**. “He will always put the interests of America first. He has my trust. He has my support. Today, he has my congratulations,” the President said in a statement.
[Read President Trump’s statement on Mike Pompeo’s confirmation.](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=f4f1a16f820f2857d54c8474691392310665fb33ce54660ad6e6646dce0c5e6e286b07eb07d5598592a3cbecf790bf2270179af19b28e0bb)
**More** : [See what makes Mike Pompeo uniquely qualified to be Secretary of State. ](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=f4f1a16f820f28573353fd64a8bbccef4395dbd8de684fa269b6f6d6f12137ca6f00e35b6b2998477c0b8d97c11020b0f1d1ee3ca425b1ab)

---
|

## What’s in your medicine cabinet?
**Each day, Americans are prescribed medications that go unused**. These left-over drugs often wind up in medicine cabinets, closets, and other spaces throughout the home. Unused or expired medicines often seem innocuous, but many contribute to accidental poisonings, overdoses, and thefts across the country.
**Tomorrow is a good day to help change that**. President Trump has announced that the 15th National Prescription Drug Take Back Day will be tomorrow, Saturday, April 28. Held twice per year, Take Back Day is an opportunity for families to dispose of expired or unused prescription drugs at [one of any thousands of drop-off sites. ](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=f4f1a16f820f285711a17ad77792a2bfb98ba20293714d7b88c83e5673862ca961567457e4bb010c3a8bd6b6eb9e350b6be16434f7e48614)
**Disposal can also be done safely through the mail**. Multiple retail pharmacies, such as Costco, CVS, and Rite Aid, sell postage-paid envelopes to mail in left-over medicines. Free [mail-in envelopes ](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=f4f1a16f820f2857d451819aa3b27d803c763da8c651c401b5bbb839a2b967661ee1fccb0b8e46771ca5fc2b565431cbcf0e618e9eb27647)are also offered by the National Safety Council.
[What’s in your medicine cabinet? Today is a good day to find out.](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=f4f1a16f820f2857979f74055120546db740d24bc45eaf6a5f3ec9870aa293381b08d599592c6b1c3ed587594c1849cd69be026a20f0b2b2)
**Watch** : [President Trump’s weekly address on Take Back Day ](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=f4f1a16f820f285736d5fc3b04994a679946820472d50733bf4cd5c7b41efb5d5f7b3ed70f88de7c9e0ffeb34ffa79bb23c17262a162c240)

---
|

## Photo of the Day
Official White House Photo by D. Myles Cullen
**President Donald J. Trump delivers remarks at the Wounded Warrior Project Soldier Ride** | April 26, 2018

[Privacy Policy](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=f4f1a16f820f285739bb2a3302c9d9180529617c09cd7115a206156a7630503635e6815d1b2cead6b8235d59c9ecf209dbb13b0b262ff6b1) | [Contact the White House](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=f4f1a16f820f2857f8302462c54b49c7c0a38b9dcfca0461c0b2414c5f33df003d47ce4465cdee2759de1c79804c7eeb174a3a5cac3ecd35) | [Unsubscribe](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/unsub_center.aspx?qs=d453c42a6560d1d4d84beea3ef57ab7a43e91bef9ff9e91abe25747641f8e0b9e3ecbf0f4021451fe667258176170bbebb1628741e6be935923f3e937c2bd0aa)
---

name: Keith Ellison
subject: You haven't donated yet this year → Will you contribute today?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Democratic National Committee, www
body:
Republicans will stop at nothing to try to outspend us at every turn -- we need to give Democrats the resources they need to win. Chip in before Monday's deadline:
---
We aren't just fighting to take back the House this year, Derek. We're fighting for the future of our country.

Trump and his Republican allies in Congress have pushed an agenda built on hatred and bigotry. They've torn at the fabric of our country just to hold on to their majorities and give their wealthy donors kickbacks.

This year is our chance to do something about it, and there's no better time to start than right now.

**That's why I'm asking you to make your first $3 contribution to the Democratic Party of 2018. Monday at midnight is an important fundraising deadline -- and your contribution will help Democrats take on the GOP in all 50 states.**

**[ DONATE: $3 NOW](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052293a/4067b0a/5eb9b7925/7e425ba4/3588767480/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/april-deadline-2018?amount=3&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=1+-+DONATE+3+NOW&utm_campaign=em_20180427_ke_nd_b&source=em_20180427_ke_nd_b&refcode=em_20180427_ke_nd_b")
[DONATE: $10 NOW](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052293a/4067b0a/5eb9b7925/7e425ba5/3588767480/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/april-deadline-2018?amount=10&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=2+-+DONATE+10+NOW&utm_campaign=em_20180427_ke_nd_b&source=em_20180427_ke_nd_b&refcode=em_20180427_ke_nd_b")
[DONATE: $25 NOW](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052293a/4067b0a/5eb9b7925/7e425ba2/3588767480/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/april-deadline-2018?amount=25&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=3+-+DONATE+25+NOW&utm_campaign=em_20180427_ke_nd_b&source=em_20180427_ke_nd_b&refcode=em_20180427_ke_nd_b")
[DONATE: $50 NOW](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052293a/4067b0a/5eb9b7925/7e425ba3/3588767480/VEsC/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/april-deadline-2018?amount=50&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=4+-+DONATE+50+NOW&utm_campaign=em_20180427_ke_nd_b&source=em_20180427_ke_nd_b&refcode=em_20180427_ke_nd_b")
[DONATE: $100 NOW](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052293a/4067b0a/5eb9b7925/7e425ba0/3588767480/VEsD/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/april-deadline-2018?amount=100&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=5+-+DONATE+100+NOW&utm_campaign=em_20180427_ke_nd_b&source=em_20180427_ke_nd_b&refcode=em_20180427_ke_nd_b")
[Or donate another amount.](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052293a/4067b0a/5eb9b7925/7e425ba1/3588767480/VEsA/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/april-deadline-2018?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=6+-+Or+donate+another+amount&utm_campaign=em_20180427_ke_nd_b&source=em_20180427_ke_nd_b&refcode=em_20180427_ke_nd_b")
** Here's what I know for sure, Derek: Republicans are going to have no problem raising money from the special interests they've been enriching over the past year. The GOP has been very good to Wall Street, billionaires, and lobbyists -- and you can expect that those folks are going to reward Republicans up and down the ticket.

But I also know that powerful grassroots organizing can overcome the GOP and their money. When Democrats have the resources we need to organize in every community across the country, we win.

What we raise before Monday's deadline will help build the national organizing effort it will take for Democrats to win in November.

**[Make your first $3 donation of 2018 before Monday's deadline and let's bring the fight to the Republican Party.](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052293a/4067b0a/5eb9b7925/7e425bae/3588767480/VEsB/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/april-deadline-2018?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=7+-++if+consinConsGroup29422+Chip+in+another&utm_campaign=em_20180427_ke_nd_b&source=em_20180427_ke_nd_b&refcode=em_20180427_ke_nd_b")**

In solidarity,

Keith

Keith Ellison
Deputy Chair
Democratic National Committee

This organization is powered by you, and we'd love to hear your ideas. Send us any comments, criticisms, or feedback [here](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052293a/4067b0a/5eb9b7925/7e425baa/3588767480/VEsHBA/ "http://my.democrats.org/page/s/contact-the-democrats?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=11+-+here&utm_campaign=em_20180427_ke_nd_b&source=em_20180427_ke_nd_b&refcode=em_20180427_ke_nd_b"), or just reply to this email! Thanks for supporting our party.

name: Joe Donnelly
subject: I won't let them cut Social Security
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Donnelly for Indiana
body:
At the end of 2017, Republicans in Congress passed their disastrous tax bill that has devastated insurance markets and is causing our national debt to skyrocket. I voted no -- it was an unquestionably |
---

| | Peter,

At the end of 2017, Republicans in Congress passed their disastrous tax bill that has devastated insurance markets and is causing our national debt to skyrocket. **I voted no -- it was an unquestionably bad bill for Hoosiers.**

Now, the nonpartisan Congressional Budget Office expects our annual federal budget deficit will reach **$1,000,000,000,000.00** by 2020 thanks to the McConnell-Ryan tax plan. And Republicans' latest solution in Indiana and Washington? "Fixing" the financial problem they themselves created by cutting Social Security benefits.

But I know how crippling Social Security cuts would be to Hoosiers. **That 's why I'll fight any proposal to weaken Social Security, cut payments, or raise the eligibility age. Period. End of story.**

**[Sign my petition to tell Mitch McConnell and Paul Ryan: You can't cut Social Security to clean up your mess.](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c20d4/7377f838/3ea3da95/51f958d4/1704532998/VEsH/ "http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/s/reject-cuts-to-social-security")** | **[**Sign the Petition**](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c20d4/7377f838/3ea3da95/51f958d4/1704532998/VEsE/ "http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/s/reject-cuts-to-social-security")**
---
Wealthy donors and special interests would love paying for their tax breaks with cuts to Social Security and Medicare -- but that's not who I serve, Peter. I'm on the side of the hardworking Hoosiers who have spent their lives paying into Social Security and expect it to be there when they retire. So if Washington tries to take away what you've earned, I won't back down. **[Add your name to tell Congress: Hoosiers won't allow ANY cuts to Social Security.](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c20d4/7377f838/3ea3da95/51f958d4/1704532998/VEsF/ "http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/s/reject-cuts-to-social-security")**
**[**Add your Name**](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c20d4/7377f838/3ea3da95/51f958d4/1704532998/VEsC/ "http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/s/reject-cuts-to-social-security")**
---
I will never give up the fight for working families, and I am thankful to have you on my side as we stand up for what is right.

All the best,
Joe

name: Jason Yates
subject: Should social media be regulated?
body:
| | We want to hear from you!
---
[ ](https://www3.myfaithvotes.org/p180427srv01?e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e180427srv01&n=1)
| Peter, I’m guessing you may have seen in the news these past few weeks the Facebook controversy and the scandal surrounding the Cambridge Analytica breach of Facebook’s users' data. Did Facebook know Cambridge Analytica had compromised users information? Were their systems and processes so weak it allowed this to happen? These are the questions the United State Congress has been trying to answer. But the investigation has brought up an even more important question many have been curious about for years – does Facebook silence conservative on their platform? You don’t have to look long or hard to find many examples that seem to prove they might. Most recently Diamond and Silk, two outspoken Pro-Trump supporters, had their Facebook content censored by Facebook who deemed their conservative messages “unsafe.” Facebook has since taken back their decision but only after tremendous public pressure. The questions many Americans are now asking, should social media sites be regulated to ensure conservative viewpoints aren’t unfairly censored? Would that solve the problem? [](https://www3.myfaithvotes.org/p180427srv01?e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e180427srv01&n=2) We want to hear from you! Social media plays an important role in our society today whether we like it or not! [Peter, we would greatly appreciate you taking just a couple minutes to answer this survey so that we know what you think about this issue and what role social media plays in your life.](https://www3.myfaithvotes.org/p180427srv01?e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e180427srv01&n=3) We seek to use social media as a powerful tool to spread Biblical values and stand for truth! But the minute social media begins censoring speech it disagrees with we enter into dangerous territory. I hope to hear from you on this issue! **United. We Stand.**

Jason Yates
CEO
[](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.myfaithvotes.org%2F&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e180427srv01&n=4)

For months, President Trump has been giving one Senate race a little more attention than any of the others.

He's been placing regular calls to Florida to chat with Rick Scott -- the sitting Republican governor.

And this month, Scott finally announced his intentions. He's challenging Bill Nelson.

Even though he's only been an official candidate for a few weeks now, Scott's associates are already telling reporters that he plans to raise $100 million to win Bill Nelson's seat. And he's already flown up to Washington to meet with conservative donors -- and start to make good on that promise.

**I want you to pay attention to how this race develops because it shows exactly what we're up against in 2018 in all our races across the country.**

body:

| **The Lesson of Squandered Savings for Teachers, Taxpayers & Kids**
Rebecca Kelley, Governor Scott's Director of Communications April 26, 2018
In case you missed it, Rebecca Kelley, Governor Scott’s Director of Communications, issued the following statement yesterday:
“Vermont cannot afford more missed opportunities.

“**Testimony in the Legislature Wednesday confirmed the concerns Governor Scott raised last year about ****[failing to take full advantage of the opportunity to save $26 million each year](https://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=fcd7928e47&e=19cebfe173)**** by transitioning to a statewide health benefit for school employees. Had they agreed to it, this plan would have allowed us to lower statewide property tax rates and invest more in education, without asking school employees to pay more.**

“Unfortunately, as the union and legislative leadership appear to be acknowledging, the complexity and inefficiency of more than 50 separately-negotiated contracts for school employee health plans has put the sustainability of those plans at significant risk. Additionally, school employees’ health insurance costs are expected to skyrocket by 16.8 percent next year (10.2 percent after use of reserves). The increasing costs also put more burden on property tax payers. The Administration predicted these outcomes last year and presented the statewide health benefit plan to prevent them.

“Again, not only could this instability and alarming increase in costs have been prevented, we could have used the savings to reduce tax rates, address the deficit in the Education Fund and invest more in early care and learning, and higher education – while making sure school employees didn’t pay more for healthcare.

“**Thankfully, it’s not too late for the Legislature to do the right thing. If they act now and implement the Governor’s plan for a single health benefit for school employees, we can protect those employees, generate significant savings for taxpayers, and start to put Vermont on a stable path to steadily invest more in education quality, without raising taxes. **

“Unfortunately, the way the legislative leadership treated this issue last year is an example of a disappointing impulse to put partisan politics ahead of progress. This year some leaders of the majority have even suggested sending the Governor legislation he has said he cannot support, for the sole purpose of generating negative news coverage ([source: Seven Days](https://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=41ce955ca6&e=19cebfe173)).

“Vermonters want elected officials to find common ground. That’s why we hope the Legislature’s majority will reconsider its approach and work with us, and the minority, to reach consensus on issues we all agree will grow our economy and make Vermont more affordable – including expanding educational options and improving quality, without raising taxes.

“**The Governor is convinced we can do this with a combination of ideas the Legislature and Administration have offered this session, including a statewide health benefit for school employees. The Administration will be presenting a path forward early next week that simultaneously stabilizes education tax rates and increases educational opportunities for our children.**

“Again, Vermont cannot afford more missed opportunities, which is exactly what they can expect if the legislative leaders treat the forthcoming proposal the way they treated the Governor’s statewide health plan for school employees.

“**We hope legislative leaders will join the Governor in putting progress first and having the courage to recognize our urgent need to move beyond the policies of the past to improve the state’s economic and demographic trajectory for the future.** ”
###
(Note: For a refresher on the Governor’s education savings proposal from last year visit [http://governor.vermont.gov/press-release/education-savings-proposal-frequently-asked-questions](https://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=a87dc00184&e=19cebfe173))

| | There are a lot of rumors churning about Trump's intention to fire special investigator Robert Mueller. There are whispers Trump wants to fire Mueller. Or Trump wants Mueller's boss at the Justice Department, Rod Rosenstein, to fire Mueller. Or Trump wants to fire Rosenstein because he won't fire Mueller and replace him with someone who will. It sounds like bad TV, I know. That's what happens when you elect a reality TV star as president. But here's the real truth: **Mueller is doing a thorough job with his investigation.** He's a decorated Marine with a long record of integrity. He's served under Republicans and Democrats. He is the right person for this job. [_**Robert Mueller should be allowed to finish his investigation without interference from President Trump or his administration. Hey there --
Wanted to make sure you saw this note about the 2020 census.
Decisions made as a result of this census will have implications for decades to come, and it's no coincidence that the communities the Trump administration's plan would sell short have a history of supporting Democrats.
[**Today is the last day to sign our petition calling for a fair and representative head count, so please click here add your name to tell the GOP not to undercount ANY American.**](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fdf65/2a380d31/b0a53d6/f18c44/1618366737/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVk1RVk5VVGtGTlJTVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IjAzMjY0In0=/ "http://petitions.signforgood.com/2020census/?code=hassan&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=03264&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fsecure.actblue.com%2Fdonate%2F2018_2020census_up%3Frefcode%3De20180426_2020censusja_up")
Thank you,
Team Maggie
_\---------------Original Message---------------_
Team --
Since our founding, America has undergone a massive headcount every decade. The census then shapes up the playing field for the country for the decade to come.
**The 2020 census results will have an enormous impact on a number of important decisions -- namely, the makeup of Congress.**
Unfortunately, the Trump administration's plan will undercount minority, immigrant, and LGBTQ people. [**If you believe that this is unacceptable and that EVERY American must be counted, please sign our petition calling for a fair and representative census.**](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fdf65/2a380d31/b0a53d6/f18c45/1618366737/VEsC/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVk1RVk5VVGtGTlJTVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IjAzMjY0In0=/ "https://petitions.signforgood.com/2020census/?code=hassan&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=03264&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fsecure.actblue.com%2Fdonate%2F2018_2020census_up%3Frefcode%3De20180426_2020censusja_up")
It's not just representation in Congress that the census impacts. Funding for public roads, schools, hospitals, and more are all also determined in part by the census. Undercounting any Granite Stater -- or American -- would put them and their families at an unfair disadvantage.
Decisions made as a result of this census will have implications for decades to come, and it's no coincidence that the communities the Trump administration's plan would sell short have a history of supporting Democrats.
[**Please sign our petition urging the Trump administration not to marginalize communities and to fairly count every single American in the census.**](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fdf65/2a380d31/b0a53d6/f18c45/1618366737/VEsD/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVk1RVk5VVGtGTlJTVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IjAzMjY0In0=/ "https://petitions.signforgood.com/2020census/?code=hassan&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=03264&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fsecure.actblue.com%2Fdonate%2F2018_2020census_up%3Frefcode%3De20180426_2020censusja_up")
Thank you for taking action,
Team Maggie

body:
Peter,
Since the start of this campaign, I’ve been talking about purchasing a used Winnebago RV (made right here in the district) and campaigning non-stop between now and Nov. 6—crisscrossing the **39 counties** that make up Iowa’s 4th District. I’m proud to say that our RV just arrived in Sioux City and will be hitting the road in the next week! Here’s a sneak peek...
I’ve been to every one of the **39 counties** in this district, most of them multiple times, and now we’re hitting them all once again! [**_Help support our 39 county tour with a gift of $39 or whatever you can today!_**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/1117401/6229720/-2142159897?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDEvMS82MTU4MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyM2M3NDQ2ZC03ZjRhLWU4MTEtODBjMy0wMDE1NWRhNzg1ZTQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=yRexjyyVF5wI6x91hM2BLHsm-MOXss0WEmgsv-85E40=)

**NH REPUBLICAN PARTY**
**WEEKLY NEWSLETTER**
**FRIDAY, APRIL 27TH**
|
---
**_A Note From Jeanie_**
**Dear Derek,**
**
**
**It’s been an exciting week here in New Hampshire. You can tell “election fever” is starting—right along with Spring fever!**
****
**On Monday, we had RNC Co-Chair Bob Paduchik in state to spend some time with some of our most ardent Trump supporters at a roundtable at the NHIOP followed by a hugely successful cigar reception at the Snow Shoe Club in Concord. Bob shared with us his optimism for the state and the nation.**
****
**On Wednesday, Rudy Giuliani visited. He is a tireless American hero and folks here gave him a warm New Hampshire welcome. He made several media stops, had a roundtable with some of NHRSC’s high dollar donors at the Bedford Village Inn, and then gave an enthusiastic speech at the Castleton in Windham to over 250 folks gathered to support the Senate PAC. He also had dinner at two of NH’s finest establishments, the Tuscan Kitchen and the Copper Door. Rudy is also a cigar enthusiast and stopped at Castro’s in Manchester to pick up some cigars for his trip back to New York. Folks, I mention all the places he visited because these establishments are all owned by folks who support the Republican party – so I encourage you to support them.**
****
**Tonight I’ll speak at the Cheshire County Republican Committee’s annual dinner in Keene and tomorrow I’ll speak with the College Republicans in Portsmouth at the 100 Club. I expect to experience the same kind of enthusiasm as I’ve experienced all week. No matter how the Democrats try to beat us down and obstruct, we won’t let them. We’ve got facts and success on our side and we need to keep talking about that with everyone.**
****
**Next week be on the lookout for my op-ed on Shahassen – as a reminder of just how badly Shaheen and Hassen are playing partisan politics at the expense of New Hampshire taxpayers.**
****

**Sincerely, **

**Jeanie**

**Op-Ed from NHYR Chair Joe Sweeney on the Second Amendment**
_For this reason, New Hampshire Republicans have defended and strengthened the Second Amendment in New Hampshire._

_In February 2017, Republicans passed and Gov. Chris Sununu signed constitutional carry, repealing the requirement to have a permit to carry in the State of New Hampshire. In fact, that was the first bill our popular governor signed into law._

_In March 2018, New Hampshire House Republicans voted down a parliamentary maneuver by Democratic lawmakers hoping to suspend House rules and introduce legislation to ban bump stocks, raise the age of purchase to 21 and curb gun rights in various other ways. When the left launched its gun-control initiative, Republicans in New Hampshire held the line. And contrary to some rumors, there will be no so-called “Red Flag” legislation as long as Republicans have a say in Concord._

clean_body: | | nh.gop NH REPUBLICAN PARTY WEEKLY NEWSLETTER FRIDAY, APRIL 27TH | A Note From Jeanie Dear Derek, It’s been an exciting week here in New Hampshire. You can tell “election fever” is starting—right along with Spring fever! ** On Monday, we had RNC Co-Chair Bob Paduchik in state to spend some time with some of our most ardent Trump supporters at a roundtable at the NHIOP followed by a hugely successful cigar reception at the Snow Shoe Club in Concord. Bob shared with us his optimism for the state and the nation. On Wednesday, Rudy Giuliani visited. He is a tireless American hero and folks here gave him a warm New Hampshire welcome. He made several media stops, had a roundtable with some of NHRSC’s high dollar donors at the Bedford Village Inn, and then gave an enthusiastic speech at the Castleton in Windham to over 250 folks gathered to support the Senate PAC. He also had dinner at two of NH’s finest establishments, the Tuscan Kitchen and the Copper Door. Rudy is also a cigar enthusiast and stopped at Castro’s in Manchester to pick up some cigars for his trip back to New York. Folks, I mention all the places he visited because these establishments are all owned by folks who support the Republican party – so I encourage you to support them. Tonight I’ll speak at the Cheshire County Republican Committee’s annual dinner in Keene and tomorrow I’ll speak with the College Republicans in Portsmouth at the 100 Club. I expect to experience the same kind of enthusiasm as I’ve experienced all week. No matter how the Democrats try to beat us down and obstruct, we won’t let them. We’ve got facts and success on our side and we need to keep talking about that with everyone. Next week be on the lookout for my op-ed on Shahassen – as a reminder of just how badly Shaheen and Hassen are playing partisan politics at the expense of New Hampshire taxpayers. Sincerely, Jeanie Join the Grassroots Circle! The Grassroots Circle is a unique way for you to get involved while supporting the Party. Becoming a member couldn't be easier -- join today for as little as $10 per month. There are three levels of support: The Grassroots Supporter, the NHGOP Advocate and the VIP Investor. Intern with the NH GOP! Are you a student or know a student that is interested in Politics? Learn the skills necessary to win elections and meet the Republican leaders that govern our State and write legislation. A packed house on Wednesday night for the Senate Republican Majority PAC with Mayor Rudy Giuliani! REPUBLICAN SPOTLIGHT Our Republican Spotlight this week is State Senator Ruth Ward! Senator Ward is a great Republican advocate in Concord, voting this past week to help pass a new $10 million school safety infrastructure fund (which some Democrats opposed). Senator Ward has done so much for the GOP, and we thank her for her leadership! TWEET OF THE WEEK Tweet With Us! | | Op-Ed from NHYR Chair Joe Sweeney on the Second Amendment For this reason, New Hampshire Republicans have defended and strengthened the Second Amendment in New Hampshire. In February 2017, Republicans passed and Gov. Chris Sununu signed constitutional carry, repealing the requirement to have a permit to carry in the State of New Hampshire. In fact, that was the first bill our popular governor signed into law. In March 2018, New Hampshire House Republicans voted down a parliamentary maneuver by Democratic lawmakers hoping to suspend House rules and introduce legislation to ban bump stocks, raise the age of purchase to 21 and curb gun rights in various other ways. When the left launched its gun-control initiative, Republicans in New Hampshire held the line. And contrary to some rumors, there will be no so-called “Red Flag” legislation as long as Republicans have a say in Concord. | **_Decision Time_** We've reached that time of year. The grass is starting to turn green again, the sun is making a reappearance, and the perennial flower known simply as the "yard sign" is blooming statewide. You know what that means -- Primary Day is just over a week away.

In fact, early voting is already underway in counties across the state. Hoosier Republicans are going into their local courthouses, libraries, town halls, and civic centers to vote in races that will select our party's nominee for U.S. Senate and many other local races.

Whether you plan to vote early or on May 8, make sure that you cast your ballot in this important election. Beyond the great feeling you'll get while sporting an "I Voted" sticker, your vote is the easiest way to make sure your voice is heard in selecting the Hoosiers who will serve us in elected office.

**Indiana Secretary of State Connie Lawson** has built out [a fantastic online portal](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=fe97e168f3&e=1acd9bb3d6) where you can see all of the candidates on your ballot and view your poling places and hours. It's a great resource, and I definitely recommend checking it out.

And if you plan to vote absentee, next Monday (April 30) is the last day to request an absentee ballot. [Click here](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=8b4ea70752&e=1acd9bb3d6) to learn more about having a ballot mailed to you.

In just a few days, we'll have our U.S. Senate nominee to take on Democrat Joe Donnelly -- and provide Hoosiers with another consistently conservative voice in Washington. And we'll have nominees for U.S. House races and critical local races.

But first you need to vote.

-Kyle Hupfer

_Pictured: Chairman Kyle Hupfer revs up the crowd at the Allen County Lincoln Day Dinner on Monday evening, prior to the event's big debate between U.S. Senate candidates Mike Braun, Luke Messer and Todd Rokita._

| **_Global Tech Firm Infosys Picks Indiana for U.S. Education Hub_**
Governor Holcomb likes to say that he's taking Indiana to the world, and bringing the world to Indiana. And yesterday, he brought the world home to Indiana in a _**BIG**_** ** way.

He joined Vice President Mike Pence and global tech firm Infosys to announce the company's plans to [locate its U.S. Education Center in Indiana](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=71d314b79a&e=1acd9bb3d6). Infosys will completely revitalize the former Indianapolis International Airport terminal site, building out its massive campus for training U.S. workers and clients with the latest technology skills. Check out [the impressive rendering](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=9b01553405&e=1acd9bb3d6).

In addition to the company's commitment last year to create 2,000 great new Hoosier jobs, Infosys is pledging to create an additional 1,000 Hoosier technology and training jobs (for a total of 3,000 Hoosier jobs).

This commitment follows Governor Holcomb's meetings with Infosys in India last year, where he personally sold company executives on the world-class, vibrant ecosystem for growth and opportunity we’ve built here in Indiana.

A big project like this is truly transformational for our state, helping build out the next generation of tech talent, and will secure Indiana's place as the Midwest's innovation and technology capital.

You know what that means -- Primary Day is just over a week away. In fact, early voting is already underway in counties across the state. Hoosier Republicans are going into their local courthouses, libraries, town halls, and civic centers to vote in races that will select our party's nominee for U.S. Senate and many other local races. Whether you plan to vote early or on May 8, make sure that you cast your ballot in this important election. Beyond the great feeling you'll get while sporting an "I Voted" sticker, your vote is the easiest way to make sure your voice is heard in selecting the Hoosiers who will serve us in elected office. Indiana Secretary of State Connie Lawson has built out where you can see all of the candidates on your ballot and view your poling places and hours. It's a great resource, and I definitely recommend checking it out.
